title: Bonfire Night Burns Bright in Britain
story: Every November 5, people across Britain light massive bonfires and set off colorful fireworks to celebrate Guy Fawkes Day! They burn stuffed dummies called "guys" on top of the fires, remembering when Guy Fawkes tried to blow up Parliament centuries ago. Kids make their own guy dolls from old clothes and newspapers, then families gather around crackling fires eating toffee apples and jacket potatoes. The night sky explodes with sparklers and rockets as everyone chants "Remember, remember the fifth of November!"
activity_question: What do people burn on top of bonfires during Guy Fawkes Day?
bonus_fact: Some towns hold competitions for the best homemade guy dummy!

title: Red Hair, Don't Care Day!
story: Redheads everywhere get to shine extra bright on November 5 during National Love Your Red Hair Day! People with fiery locks celebrate by posting selfies, wearing their favorite red outfits, and sharing stories about their unique hair color. Friends and family join in by complimenting the redheads in their lives and learning cool facts about red hair. Some schools even have special red-themed dress-up days where everyone wears red to show support!
activity_question: How do people typically celebrate National Love Your Red Hair Day?
activity_choices: By posting selfies and wearing red outfits
By hiding their red hair under hats
activity_answer: By posting selfies and wearing red outfits
bonus_fact: Only about 2% of people worldwide have natural red hair!
